steps.title: Convert the percent loss to a decay factor
steps.content: Losing $18\%$ of the mass each period leaves $100\%-18\%=82\%$, so the per-period multiplier is $0.82$ — not $0.18$.

steps.title: Set up the exponent
steps.content: One decay period equals $9.09$ minutes, so after $t$ minutes the number of completed periods is $\dfrac{t}{9.09}$. The model is $$M=100\,(0.82)^{t/9.09}.$$ Check: at $t=9.09$, $M=100(0.82)^{1}=82$ grams, an $18\%$ drop, as required.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M=100(0.18)^{t+9.09}$ — uses the wrong base ($0.18$ instead of $0.82$) and a nonsensical exponent. Eliminated.
  • B) $M=100(0.18)^{t/9.09}$ — correct exponent form but wrong base; would lose $82\%$ per period. Eliminated.
  • C) $M=100(0.82)^{t+9.09}$ — correct base but the exponent $t+9.09$ has the wrong units and gives the wrong value at $t=0$. Eliminated.
  • D) $M=100(0.82)^{t/9.09}$ — correct base and correct exponent.
That is the correct answer.
